summary: Closed the two bounded verification gaps by adding a scripted controller/app-server active-shell recovery journey and a managed-session request-tracking replay regression.

gapStatuses.evidence: test_pr_resolver_scripted_app_server_journey_resumes_active_shell drives CodexSessionAdapter through DockerCodexManagedSessionController and its app-server invoke boundary. The first completed response contains an outer completed tool wrapper whose nested payload identifies a still-running shell session; terminal evidence is absent. The deterministic continuation stays on the same session, epoch, and thread and the second scripted app-server turn writes var/pr_resolver/result.json, producing a recovered outcome. The existing exhaustion test remains at the same adapter boundary.

gapStatuses.requirement: Activity retry or Temporal replay cannot create duplicate continuation turns
gapStatuses.evidence: test_agent_session_replay_deduplicates_terminal_contract_continuation restores the deterministic terminal-contract request ID as completed request-tracking state, retries the same send-follow-up update, and proves no agent_runtime activity is invoked and the compact tracking entry remains unchanged.

targetedLocalChecks.result: 100 targeted Python tests passed with two pre-existing asyncio-marker warnings; the automatic frontend phase passed 980 tests with 238 skipped.
